fundamentals.name: Adobe Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Adobe Inc. operates as a technology company worldwide. The Digital Media segment offers products and services that enable individuals, teams, and enterprises to create, publish, and promote content. This segment serves photographers, video editors, graphic and experience designers, game developers, content creators, students, marketers, business owners, knowledge workers, and consumers. The Digital Experience segment provides an integrated platform; and products, services, and solutions that enable brands and businesses to create, manage, execute, measure, monetize, and optimize customer experiences from analytics to commerce. This segment serves marketers, advertisers, agencies, publishers, merchandisers, merchants, web analysts, data scientists, developers, and executives across the C-suite. The Publishing and Advertising segment offers e-learning, technical document publishing, web conferencing, document and forms platform, web application development, printing, and Adobe Advertising solutions. It also provides consulting, training, customer management, technical support, and learning services. The company offers its solutions to enterprise customers, and businesses and consumers; and licenses its products to end-user customers through app stores and website at adobe.com. It markets and distributes its products through distributors, retailers, software developers, mobile app stores, systems integrators, independent software vendors, value-added resellers, and original equipment and hardware manufacturers. The company also provides an online visibility management and content marketing software-as-a-service platform. The company has a strategic alliance with HUMAIN for the development of generative AI models and AI-powered applications. The company was formerly known as Adobe Systems Incorporated and changed its name to Adobe Inc. in October 2018. Adobe Inc. was founded in 1982 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Adobe is a high-margin, rapidly growing software company with a strong balance sheet and attractive valuation metrics.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 3.2%; next earnings date is 2026-06-11.
positives: Revenue growth 12.0% YoY and earnings growth 11.1% YoY indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin of 89.4%, operating margin of 38.8%, and profit margin of 29.5% demonstrate excellent profitability.
Return on equity at 58.8% and return on assets at 18.9% show efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow of $9.32B TTM highlights robust cash generation capabilities.
Forward P/E of 9.82x and PEG ratio of 0.69x suggest the stock is attractively valued relative to its growth prospects.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 58.34% is moderate, though manageable given cash flow.
Current ratio of 0.91 and quick ratio of 0.79 indicate tight short-term liquidity.
The company's own AI tools are reportedly impacting its stock photo business.
Beta of 1.42 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Adobe's own AI tools are reportedly impacting its stock photo business, a concern management has acknowledged. This comes as the company is also described as future-proofing with AI agents and is being compared favorably against Autodesk in creative software. Despite the AI-related headwinds in its stock photo segment, Adobe's overall financial health remains robust, with strong free cash flow generation.
